message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_entr composes through nested-where for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_entr had a fail-loud guard rejecting tracked inputs because
the body extracted values and rebuilt a fresh requires_grad=false
leaf. PyTorch's torch.special.entr is differentiable on the open
positive half-line. Same severed-autograd pattern fix as recent
xlogy / xlog1py / logit / digamma rounds.

Composition (nested where, autograd-aware all the way down):

    entr(x) = where(x < 0, -inf,
                   where(x == 0, 0,
                         -x * log(x)))

Implemented via tensor_neg + tensor_log + tensor_mul +
tensor_eq + tensor_lt + tensor_where. The chain rule on the smooth
branch yields the analytical derivative

    d entr / dx | x > 0 = -log(x) - 1

while the masks at x == 0 and x < 0 route the output through
constants, blocking gradient flow on those positions (returns 0
rather than the undefined +inf limit at x=0+, consistent with the
constant-branch backward semantics elsewhere in tensor_where).

Tests:
  entr_propagates_gradients_on_positive_branch: input
    [0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0] under sum loss; asserts grad equals
    -log(x) - 1 element-wise.
  entr_zero_and_negative_branches_block_gradient: mixed input
    [0.0, -1.0, 0.5] confirms backward doesn't panic on the
    masked-inf branches.

Existing entr_basic forward test continues to pass — forward parity
preserved bit-for-bit at all three branches.

Files closure for one slice of frankentorch-1tax (umbrella
"statistical/special ops with severed-autograd" tracking bead).

Workspace: 1125 ft-api tests green (1123 + 2 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
